seoTitle: How to Save Emails From Outlook to OneDrive
metaDescription: Learn how to save emails and attachments from Outlook to OneDrive, using quick cloud-save toggles, message export, and the web print-to-PDF method.
intro: Backing up important invoices or client threads locally wastes drive space and makes files hard to find later. Outlook's integration with OneDrive lets you save attachments and full message bodies straight to the cloud. This guide covers three ways to do it, from quick toggles to exporting full messages.

steps.heading: Save a single attachment
steps.body: Hover over a received file attachment and click the drop-down Save to OneDrive option.

steps.heading: Save all attachments at once
steps.body: Click the Save all to OneDrive action link at the top of the message to move every attachment at once.

steps.heading: Save the full message body
steps.body: Double-click a message to open it fully, click the File tab, then choose Save As from the left navigation.

steps.heading: Pick a file format and folder
steps.body: Select Outlook Message Format or a text file type from the dropdown, choose your synced OneDrive folder, and hit Save.

steps.heading: Use print-to-PDF from the web app
steps.body: In the browser version, click the three dots next to the message header, choose Print, and select Save as PDF to your OneDrive directory.

steps.heading: Verify the backup
steps.body: Open your OneDrive dashboard in the browser and confirm your newly archived files loaded correctly.

faq.question: Can I back up an entire email conversation, not just attachments?
faq.answer: Yes, using File then Save As lets you export the full message body in Outlook Message Format or as text.

faq.question: Does this work in both the desktop app and the browser?
faq.answer: Yes, though the browser version relies more on the Print to PDF workflow instead of the Save As menu.

faq.question: Will saved emails automatically sync if I edit the local folder?
faq.answer: If the destination folder is a synced OneDrive folder, changes will sync automatically once your device processes them.

faq.question: How do I confirm a file actually made it to OneDrive?
faq.answer: Open your OneDrive storage dashboard in a browser and check that the newly archived file appears in the target folder.
